metadata.description: M12: Local-to-Global Consistency — Do Partial Matches Extend?

verdict.interpretation: Full-agreement pairs are massively enriched over random null (null produces zero full-agreement pairs by Monte Carlo). Congruences are a structured, non-random phenomenon. The local sufficiency threshold varies with ell, suggesting the local-to-global transition rate depends on the residual characteristic. At ell=3, the full-25 threshold is k=19 (76%), meaning pairs agreeing at 19+ of 25 primes have >50% chance of full congruence — dramatically below the null threshold of k=25. Partial agreement DOES extend.
